_meta.status: DORMANT — conceptually sound, no experimental validation
_meta.reawaken_if: Real data obtained for any domain below; domain expert collaborator available; post-CoDaWork bandwidth permits
_meta.cip_warning: Grok's CIP citations in these proposals are INCORRECT — Grok used fabricated rules, not canonical CIP. See Grok Assessment report Section 3.

southmedic_respiratory.contact_basis: Peter Higgins has existing relationship from Fuji/X-ray installation era
southmedic_respiratory.application: Respiratory gas-mix monitoring: O2/CO2/N2/anaesthetic agent compositions as D-part simplex. EITT monitors temporal stability of gas delivery. Drift flag = delivery anomaly.
southmedic_respiratory.huf_alignment: HIGH — direct analogue of EMBER energy-mix monitoring. D components summing to 100%, time-series monitoring for drift.
southmedic_respiratory.data_needed: Time-series of gas composition readings from clinical monitors during procedures
southmedic_respiratory.sterile_processing: Enzymatic detergent concentrations as compositions during wash cycles. EITT monitors process consistency.
southmedic_respiratory.readiness: CONCEPT ONLY — no worked example exists. Do not send business proposal until at least one gas-mix EITT run is completed.
southmedic_respiratory.priority: HIGHEST among medical proposals — most commercially immediate, clearest HUF alignment

blood_composition_cbc.application: Differential white cell counts (neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, eosinophils, basophils) as D=5 composition. Shannon entropy of differential = diversity measure. EITT monitors temporal stability of patient haematological state.
blood_composition_cbc.huf_alignment: HIGH — CBC differential is a classic simplex composition
blood_composition_cbc.data_needed: Serial CBC differentials from longitudinal patient cohorts (e.g., chemotherapy monitoring, ICU stays)
blood_composition_cbc.literature_note: CoDa methods in haematology are sparse. Gloor et al. (2017) established CoDa in microbiome; similar adoption path for blood.

microbiome_hmp.application: 16S rRNA OTU abundance tables as high-dimensional compositions on simplex. EITT monitors temporal stability of microbiome state. F17 provides contamination/dysbiosis early warning.
microbiome_hmp.huf_alignment: HIGH — OTU tables are the canonical example of high-dimensional compositional data
microbiome_hmp.coda_literature: Gloor et al. (2017) — established CoDa for microbiome. EITT adds temporal stability dimension.
microbiome_hmp.comparison_tool: QIIME2 — EITT + CIP could serve as a post-processing integrity complement
microbiome_hmp.data_needed: Longitudinal 16S OTU tables from HMP (publicly available)
microbiome_hmp.readiness: CONCEPT + public data available. Could be EXP-13 if Peter decides to extend chain.
microbiome_hmp.priority: SECOND HIGHEST — data is free, CoDa community already engaged with microbiome

drug_formulation.application: API + excipient compositions in pharmaceutical formulations as simplex data. EITT monitors stability over shelf life. Aligned with ICH Q1A stability guidelines.
drug_formulation.huf_alignment: MODERATE — formulation stability is compositional, but time series may be short
drug_formulation.data_needed: Stability study composition data at multiple time points (ICH standard timepoints)
drug_formulation.readiness: CONCEPT ONLY — needs pharmaceutical industry collaborator
drug_formulation.priority: LOW — long regulatory pathway, needs industry partner

pharmacokinetics.application: Multi-compartment PK systems as compositions: fraction of drug in plasma/tissue/elimination at each timepoint. EITT monitors compartment balance stability.
pharmacokinetics.cip_role: CIP framed as post-processing integrity layer (NOTE: Grok used wrong CIP rules)
pharmacokinetics.huf_alignment: MODERATE — PK is compositional but data is sparse (few timepoints per patient)
pharmacokinetics.priority: LOW — sparse data challenge for EITT

pharmacodynamics.application: Fractional receptor occupancy, % inhibition, biomarker response, tumor cell fractions (viable/apoptotic/necrotic) as compositions.
pharmacodynamics.huf_alignment: MODERATE — cell viability assays produce simplex data

toxicology.application: Metabolite profiling (parent/metabolites composition), organ distribution fractions, cytotoxicity assays (% viable/apoptosis/necrosis), API impurity profiles.
toxicology.huf_alignment: MODERATE — impurity profiles are simplex data

pulmonary_function.application: Gas exchange ratios (O2/CO2/N2) during respiratory function tests

summary.common_thread: All proposals involve compositions on a simplex monitored over time — the exact HUF use case. None have been tested with real data.
summary.action_required: Real data + worked example for at least one domain before any commercial engagement
